thesis: The FR network has already proven the playbook works: sargasses-martinique.com ranks pos 1.7 on "sargasse martinique en temps réel" and converts at 21.7% CTR on its homepage (a +15pt lift over the position-expected curve) — that is authority and intent-match, not luck. The 10× is NOT hiding in the mature FR head terms; it is in the EN/ES sites, which sit in striking-distance purgatory (Florida pos 21.7, Punta Cana pos 13.4, Cancún home pos 51 but its /sargazo-salud-riesgos/ already at pos 8.1 with zero clicks captured) against a Spanish "sargazo" demand pool of 202 queries that is almost entirely un-clicked. The strategy is therefore three compounding levers, all GitHub-Actions-automated and honesty-bound: (1) drag EN/ES head + long-tail from pos 15-50 into the top 3 by porting the exact page archetypes that already win in FR, (2) flood untapped per-beach and intent long-tail with programmatic pages built from the live region JSON (data we already publish, so zero fabrication), and (3) transfer the FR sites' earned authority to the EN/ES siblings via a deliberate cross-network link map plus fresh backlinks. "Le Veilleur" — measured, never guessed, with published backtest accuracy and per-regime reliability — is the trust moat that lets these pages out-rank institutional NOAA/USF pages and blog spam on the exact "is there sargassum today" intent.

perSite.currentState: Dominant and converting. Home pos 4.7 with 21.7% CTR (+15pt over expected) = top authority node of the network. 'sargasse martinique en temps réel' pos 1.7 (344 clicks/678 impr), 'sargasse martinique aujourd'hui' pos 2.7 (252 clicks), 'meteo sargasse martinique' pos 2.7. 75 SEO URLs, EN home already pos 7.6 at 15.7% CTR. Mostly out of opportunity-room on head terms (winCount 8, opportunityCount 0).
bigMoves: Use it as the authority donor: add contextual in-content links FR home + high-traffic pages → /en/ and the EN/ES sibling domains (sargassummiami.com etc.) to pass PageRank and intent relevance.

Defend and deepen long-tail: it already has per-commune pages (sargasses-le-diamant, sargasses-sainte-anne) — extend the same template to every remaining MQ commune/beach not yet covered (programmatic, capped 3/run).

Push 'sargasse martinique' (pos 3.5) and 'meteo sargasse martinique' (pos 2.7) to pos 1 via title/intro freshness (live date + today's clean-beach count from pipeline) and internal links from the winning real-time page.
Harvest the EN home (pos 7.6, 15.7% CTR) — it proves English intent exists even on the FR domain; route that EN equity toward the dedicated EN sites.

Has its own commune pages (deshaies, gosier, saint-francois, bouillante) and EN/best-beaches-guadeloupe.
bigMoves: Fix tracking/coverage first (empty query map in snapshot suggests a GSC property or sitemap gap) so we can target precisely.
Mirror MQ's authority-donor role toward EN/ES siblings.
Complete commune/beach long-tail coverage for Basse-Terre + Grande-Terre beaches not yet paged.
Lift 'sargasses guadeloupe' from pos 4.6 toward top 3 with the same freshness + internal-link recipe that took MQ to pos 1.7.

Receive authority links from FR anchors + new US backlinks (travel/Reddit/forum/Florida tourism).

Lock winning A/B title variants; freeze the freshness + link recipe into the weekly automation so growth keeps compounding without manual work.

x10Model:
Honest, math-backed. We separate the three compounding sources and state assumptions; 10× is a network-wide multiple on the EN/ES sites, NOT on the already-mature FR head terms.

ASSUMPTIONS (stated): CTR-by-position uses the AWR-style curve already used in seo-ctr-diagnostic (pos ~13-21 ≈ 0.5-2% CTR; pos 8 ≈ ~3-4%; pos 3 ≈ ~10-13%; pos 1-2 ≈ ~20-30%, consistent with our own MQ home at 21.7% pos 4.7). We do NOT assume we beat the curve on new pages (the MQ +15pt lift is upside we don't bank).

LEVER 1 — Striking-distance lift on existing EN/ES pages. Florida home pos 21.7 (47 impr, 3 clicks ≈ 6% — anomalously high on tiny n, treat as noise). Realistic: these sites collectively have a few hundred impressions today at ~1% effective CTR. Moving Florida 21.7→3, Punta Cana 13.4→3, Cancún 51→~10 takes per-page CTR from ~0.5-1% to ~5-12%, i.e. a 6-12× lift on the SAME impressions — before any new impression supply. On current low base this is large in % but small in absolute clicks; it's the ignition, not the engine.

LEVER 2 — New impression supply from programmatic long-tail (the real engine). The ES 'sargazo' pool alone = 202 queries almost entirely un-clicked. Per-beach × intent pages (12 beaches × 3 EN/ES regions × ~4 intent templates 'today/season/best/this-week' + the ES sargazo info cluster) = on the order of 150-300 new ranked pages. If even 60% reach top-10 (credible: thin competition is institutional NOAA/USF + blogs, and we have unique live data + freshness) at a conservative 50-200 impr/page/mo and ~4-6% CTR, that is roughly 150 pages × ~120 impr × ~5% ≈ ~900+ incremental clicks/mo on top of a current EN/ES base in the low tens. That alone is the bulk of a 10×.

LEVER 3 — Authority transfer + backlinks. FR anchors convert at proven top-3 authority (MQ home 21.7% CTR). Internal links from FR home/winning pages + sibling hreflang links raise the EN/ES sites' baseline rankings by an estimated few positions network-wide, which in the CTR curve's steep zone (pos 8→4) roughly doubles CTR again. Backlinks accelerate indexation and defend the gains. Multiplicatively: Lever 1 (6-12× on existing) compounds with Lever 2 (the new-page volume that dwarfs today's base) and Lever 3 (a ~1.5-2× ranking-driven CTR boost) → a network-level ~10× on EN/ES organic clicks within ~90 days is the central, defensible estimate. FR sites add only incremental tail gains (~10-20%) since they're already near-saturated.

WHAT MAKES IT FALL SHORT (honest): (a) indexation lag — Google may take weeks to crawl/rank the flood, pushing the 10× past 90 days; (b) thin programmatic pages getting filtered as low-value if the live-data differentiation isn't visibly strong per page (mitigated by per-beach Score + forecast = genuinely unique content); (c) seasonality — sargassum demand is peak Apr-Sep; launching into low season shrinks the pool; (d) cannibalization if 'today' and 'this-week' pages fight each other (mitigated by the cannibalization scanner); (e) the small-n noise in current numbers means today's base is uncertain, so the multiple is on a soft denominator. Realistic floor if 2-3 of these bite: ~3-5×. Realistic ceiling if we also beat the CTR curve like MQ does: >10×.
kpis: Network organic clicks/mo (GSC) — segmented FR vs EN vs ES; primary 10× target lives on EN+ES.
Per-site avg position on tracked head terms (GSC, via seo-position-tracker.cjs): Florida 21.7→≤3, Punta Cana 13.4→≤3, Cancún 51→≤10, sargazo-salud 8.1→≤5.
Count of EN/ES queries with ≥1 click/mo (today the ES 'sargazo' 202-pool is ~all zero-click — track the un-zeroing).
Indexed page count per domain (GSC Coverage) — track the programmatic flood actually getting indexed, not just built.
CTR vs expected-CTR-curve gap per top-10 page (seo-ctr-diagnostic.cjs) — replicate MQ home's +15pt lift; flag pages ranking but under-clicking.
Striking-distance page count (pos 4-15) moving into top-3 week over week.
Long-tail clicks share: % of clicks from queries outside the top-10 head terms (proves the tail is firing).
Organic → PASS conversion by site (GA4 + Mollie) — traffic that doesn't convert is vanity; watch EN/ES especially.
Orphan + cannibalization counts (must trend to ~0 as volume grows).
risks: Programmatic-content filtering: hundreds of templated pages risk being seen as thin/spam. Mitigation: every page carries genuinely unique live data (per-beach Score, status, 7-day forecast) and the honesty/backtest framing — keep pages data-rich, never boilerplate; ramp the generator gradually, watch indexation.
Seasonality: sargassum search demand peaks Apr-Sep; a flood shipped in low season ranks but earns few clicks until the season returns — the 10× may realize on a lag.

Authority dilution: aggressively linking FR anchors out to siblings can leak too much equity or look like a link scheme if over-done. Mitigation: contextual, relevant, capped link counts; favor topical relevance over volume.

Honesty doctrine vs scale pressure: as pages multiply, the temptation to fabricate testimonials/global accuracy % grows. Hard rule (already in the codebase): per-regime reliability + published backtest only, never invented numbers — a single fake number can torch the trust moat that lets us beat NOAA.
GSC small-n noise: current EN/ES baselines (3-47 impr) are statistically thin, so the 10× denominator is soft — measure on rolling 28-90d windows, not single snapshots.

GitHub Actions quota: weekly-seo-automation was already cut Mon+Thu→Mon for budget; a programmatic ramp adds runtime. Mitigation: batch the flood into a few dispatch runs, then return to cadence; reuse existing concurrency guards.
Backlink outreach deliverability/reputation (same shared-domain email concerns flagged for cold-lead reengage) — keep outreach volume sane and personalized.
Cannibalization between 'today' / 'this-week' / 'season' templates on the same beach — must run seo-cannibalization.cjs every cycle and consolidate.
quickWins: De-orphan sargassumcancun.com/sargazo-salud-riesgos/ — it already ranks pos 8.1 with ZERO clicks captured; add internal links + a clear CTA + freshness title. Free top-10 traffic the moment it's wired.

Turn on live-date + today's-clean-beach-count in the title/meta of the 3 EN/ES homes (freshTitles already true) so 'today/hoy' queries get an exact-match snippet — the single biggest CTR lever at this intent.

Ship the 3 already-defined archetype routes (season / best-beaches / this-week) as real pages on Florida, Punta Cana, Cancún — the routes exist in region JSON but the pages are stubs; this is content the generator can produce immediately.
Wire the pre-designed cross-site authority link map into the FR templates → push MQ/GP equity to the EN/ES siblings in one build.
Submit every EN/ES + beach URL via existing seo-submit-urls.cjs + submit-indexnow.cjs to force fast (re)indexation.
Fix the GP / EN-ES GSC coverage gap (empty query maps) so the weekly automation can actually target — you can't optimize what you can't see.

Generate the first batch of high-intent per-beach pages for the 3 ES/EN flagship beaches each (Miami Beach, Bávaro, Cancún/Playa del Carmen) within the current 3/run generator cap — start the long-tail engine immediately.
